headline: Nearly 1 Billion Gain Clean Water Access Since 2015
meta_description: A UN report reveals dramatic global progress: nearly 1 billion people now have access to clean drinking water, with electricity reaching 92% of the world.
summary_short: Since 2015, nearly 1 billion people have gained access to clean drinking water, and electricity now reaches 92% of the global population.
body:
A new UN report documents a remarkable shift in global living conditions over the past decade. Nearly one billion people have gained access to clean drinking water for the first time, while a similar number—1.2 billion—have acquired proper sanitation infrastructure. These gains represent a fundamental improvement in health and dignity for entire communities across the developing world.

Electricity access has expanded with equal momentum, now reaching 92 percent of the world's population. The combination of these three advances—water, sanitation, and power—creates a foundation for education, healthcare, and economic opportunity that compounds over generations. This progress reflects decades of global commitment to universal basic services, demonstrating that large-scale human problems can yield to sustained effort and investment.
